title: croatian yukon gold potatoes crumble
description: a winey, crumbly, show-off croatian dish featuring yukon gold potatoes, caramelized to perfection. plant-based-friendly and sure to impress
instructions: in a large bowl, toss the sliced potatoes with 2 tbsp of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
spread the potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
in a large skillet, heat the remaining 2 tbsp of olive oil over medium heat. add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is caramelized and golden brown, about 20-25 minutes.
add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
add the white wine to the skillet and cook, stirring to deglaze the bottom, until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, about 5 minutes.
in a separate bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs, nutritional yeast, and a pinch of salt and pepper.

in a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of potatoes followed by a layer of the breadcrumb mixture, and then a layer of vegan grated cheese. repeat this process until all of the ingredients have been used, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
bake the crumble in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are heated through.
remove the crumble from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the crumble before serving.
serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
